sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, William, presented with a complaint of right knee pain after injuring it at a trampoline park one week ago.
He described hearing a "snap" and feeling instant pain.
He reports that he does not go to the trampoline park often and this was his first time.
He has not been consistent with exercise and tries to do so for fun.
The pain is located on the outside of the knee and he has tried taking Tylenol, which did not provide relief.
He has elevated the knee and applied ice, but that's about it.
Past medical history: The patient has hypertension and is currently on 20mg of lisinopril.
He has not been taking his medication regularly, but remembers to take it most days.
He has diabetes and is on metformin 500mg.
He has not been checking his blood sugar levels regularly, aiming to do so 4-5 times a week.
He has not been focused on a specific diet and is trying to watch his carb intake.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: Lisinopril 20mg, metformin 500mg.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 180/80.
Physical exam: The patient's heart rate and rhythm are regular.
The lungs are clear bilaterally.
The knee has ecchymosis and edema on the lateral aspect, but no effusion.
The patient has a slight limp and can bear weight, but has a noticeable gait.
Range of motion is decent, but movement is painful.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a lateral ligament strain of the right knee.
Problem list: Right knee pain (active); hypertension (suspected); diabetes (active).
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg daily for the patient's right knee pain and swelling.
I referred him to physical therapy to help strengthen the muscles around the area and prevent further injury.
For his hypertension, I will continue lisinopril 20mg and order an echo to assess for any underlying issues.
For his diabetes, I will order an A1C and lipid panel to get a better understanding of his long-term condition.
Follow-up: I will review the results of the right knee x-ray, which showed no evidence of fracture or bony abnormality, and discuss further management with the patient.
I will also discuss the results of the echo, A1C, and lipid panel with the patient.
